action.reasoning: Observation: the 80-epoch h192/l4 PairMPNN achieved best MAE 0.0642; validation plateaued around epochs 74-80 but per-epoch noise remains and only one random seed has been trained. Analysis: a different seed with the same strong architecture is low-risk and likely yields partially independent errors; ensembling two same-architecture checkpoints can improve MAE more reliably than a single capacity change. Decision: update predict.py to support both normal and ensemble checkpoints, train a second h192/l4 80-epoch model with a different seed, build a two-member ensemble checkpoint, smoke-test it, and submit the ensemble for evaluation.
action.command:
python train.py --epochs 80 --batch_size 256 --hidden_dim 192 --num_layers 4 --num_rbf 48 --lr 8e-4 --seed 123 --out checkpoints/pairmpnn_h192_l4_e80_s123.pt && python - <<'PY'
import os, torch
os.makedirs('checkpoints', exist_ok=True)
torch.save({'members':[{'checkpoint':'checkpoints/pairmpnn_h192_l4_e80.pt'},{'checkpoint':'checkpoints/pairmpnn_h192_l4_e80_s123.pt'}]}, 'checkpoints/ensemble_h192_l4_e80_s42_s123.pt')
PY
python predict.py --data_path smoke --checkpoint checkpoints/ensemble_h192_l4_e80_s42_s123.pt --output smoke/out_ens.json
